title: Clean venv on Ascend hosts missing implicit CANN toolkit Python dependencies
problem_description: The CANN toolchain ships Python wheels (`auto_tune`, `te`, `hccl`, `opc_tool`) that declare runtime dependencies (`decorator`, `attrs`, `psutil`, `scipy`, `cloudpickle`, `ml-dtypes`, `tornado`, `absl-py`) not pre-installed in a clean venv. Additionally, `torch_npu` requires `pyyaml` at import time (for its memory visualization module). When any of these are missing, torch_npu initialization fails with `ModuleNotFoundError`, blocking all NPU operations.
rough_fix_approach: 1. After creating venv and installing torch-npu, scan system CANN packages for their declared dependencies: `pip show auto_tune te hccl opc_tool 2>/dev/null | grep 'Requires:'`. 2. Install missing dependencies in one shot: `pip install decorator attrs psutil scipy cloudpickle ml-dtypes tornado absl-py pyyaml`. 3. Pre-install these before first `python -c 'import torch_npu'` to avoid runtime failures. Alternative: parse `/usr/local/Ascend/ascend-toolkit/latest/` metadata to build a deterministic `cann-deps.txt`.
artifact_evidence: raw/phase_2_venv_create_attempt0.json:line 8 (Phase 2 took 280s — dependency gap filling consumed time)
reports/SUMMARY_REPORT.md — Challenges Encountered, item 4: 'CANN toolkit Python dependencies: Clean venv lacked decorator, attrs, psutil, etc.'
reports/TOOLS_EXECUTION_REPORT.md — Missing CANN Toolkit Dependencies section

involved_code_files.role: Entry script — import torch_npu triggers transitive loading of CANN Python modules (auto_tune, te, hccl) which require the missing dependencies

reasoning: This is a systematic issue for any clean venv on Ascend hosts. The dependencies are not documented in project requirements.txt — they are implicit requirements of the system CANN toolchain. The fix is straightforward (install the missing packages) but discovering them requires either error-driven iteration or prior knowledge. Capturing this as a reusable skill saves future migration runs from the same trial-and-error cycle.
